tools.name: semgrep
tools.scope: Security scanning - Go, Python, YAML, TypeScript

linting.notes: Primary linting is Go import organization. Python linting via pre-commit hooks for examples and training scripts. Semgrep provides security scanning across all languages.

env_vars: TEST_OUTPUT_DIR - Output directory for test logs
TEST_RAY_IMAGE - Optional Ray image for RayCluster
TEST_TIER - Test tier filter (Smoke, Sanity, Tier1, Tier2, Tier3, Pre-Upgrade, Post-Upgrade)
KUBECONFIG - Kubernetes cluster access (required for integration tests)
ODH_NAMESPACE - Namespace where ODH components are installed
FMS_HF_TUNING_IMAGE - Image tag for PyTorchJob model training
HF_TOKEN - HuggingFace token for restricted models
AWS_DEFAULT_ENDPOINT, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_STORAGE_BUCKET - S3 storage for datasets/models
testing.fixtures_location: tests/*/resources/ directories contain Python training scripts, MNIST downloads, etc.
testing.mock_patterns: Uses gomega matchers and test namespaces. Tests create Kubernetes resources via client-go.
testing.notes: Tests are primarily integration tests requiring a Kubernetes cluster (OpenShift/OCP/CRC) with OpenDataHub or RHOAI installed. Unit tests in tests/common/support/ can run without cluster access. Tests can be compiled with 'go test -c' for distribution. Test image uses gotestsum runner.

ci.notes: GitHub Actions verify-imports workflow gates on PR/push for Go file changes. Extensive Tekton pipelines build training runtime images (CUDA, ROCm variants) with security scanning. ODH release workflow compiles tests into binaries for distribution. No tests are executed in GitHub Actions CI - tests run in dedicated test environments with cluster access.

build.notes: This is a test suite repository, not a service. Build outputs are compiled test binaries and container images. Training runtime images built via Tekton use Python with PyTorch/CUDA/ROCm.

conventions.test_naming: Go tests use Test* prefix. Tests tagged with Tags(t, Sanity, MultiNode(3)) for tier filtering.
conventions.import_style: Go imports organized via openshift-goimports (groups standard, external, k8s.io, internal). Python uses isort with black profile.
conventions.notes: Test organization: tests/common (shared utilities), tests/kfto (KubeFlow Training Operator), tests/fms (Foundation Model Stack), tests/odh (OpenDataHub), tests/trainer (Trainer v2). Resources in tests/*/resources/ subdirectories.

lint_commands.notes: Passed - Go imports are properly organized

test_commands.notes: Passed - 30 unit tests passed in 0.020s. These tests mock Kubernetes resources and don't require cluster access.
Passed - Test compilation successful, 79MB binary created

container_recipe.notes: Python linting requires separate Python environment with pre-commit, black, isort, flake8 installed. Full integration tests require access to Kubernetes cluster with ODH/RHOAI. Unit tests in tests/common/support work without cluster. Test image (images/tests/Dockerfile) uses golang:1.24 with oc CLI and gotestsum.

results.command: apt-get update && apt-get install -y make git && go mod download

gaps: No golangci-lint configuration found - only import organization is checked

Most tests are integration tests requiring Kubernetes cluster with ODH/RHOAI - cannot be validated locally without cluster access
No unit tests for training Python scripts - Python files in tests/*/resources/ are integration test fixtures
Tests require external dependencies: S3 storage, HuggingFace tokens, pre-trained models, GPU nodes for CUDA/ROCm tests
